title: Viral Respiratory Pathogens Toolkit for Nursing Homes | LTCFs | CDC
text:
mmunity by sharing and posting communication materials such as handouts , posters , mask graphics and signs . 
Encourage visitors with respiratory symptoms to delay non-urgent in-person visitation until they are no longer infectious. Following close contact with someone with SARS-CoV-2 infection, visitors should wear a mask while in the facility. 
Keep Reading 
Long-Term Care Respiratory Illness Handout (color template) Ventilate 
In consultation with facility engineers, explore options to improve ventilation delivery and indoor air quality in resident rooms and all shared spaces. 
Test and treat 
Develop plans to provide rapid clinical evaluation and intervention to ensure residents receive timely antiviral treatment and/or prophylaxis when indicated. 
Ensure access to respiratory viral testing with rapid results (i.e., onsite or send-out testing with results available within 24 hours). Testing results can inform recommended treatment and IPC actions. 
Establish pharmacy connections to enable the use of any available respiratory virus treatments or prophylaxis. 
Respond to first signs or symptoms 
When an infection due to a respiratory virus is suspected in a resident or HCP, it is important to take rapid action to prevent the spread to others in the facility.
